CREATE TABLE interval_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, duration INTERVAL HOUR TO SECOND);
INSERT INTO interval_test VALUES (1, INTERVAL '1:30:45'), (2, INTERVAL '0:15:30'), (3, INTERVAL '2:45:00');
SELECT id, duration FROM interval_test WHERE duration > INTERVAL '1:00:00';
SELECT id, duration + INTERVAL '0:30:00' AS new_duration FROM interval_test WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_time TIMESTAMP, duration INTERVAL HOUR TO SECOND);
SELECT e.event_id, e.start_time + e.duration AS end_time FROM events e WHERE e.start_time < '2023-10-01 12:00:00';
SELECT id, duration - INTERVAL '0:15:00' AS shortened_duration FROM interval_test WHERE id = 2;
SELECT e.event_id, e.start_time + INTERVAL '1:45:00' AS new_start_time FROM events e JOIN interval_test it ON it.id = e.event_id WHERE e.event_id = 1;
SELECT (SELECT AVG(duration) FROM interval_test) AS avg_duration, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events) AS event_count;
SELECT id, duration FROM interval_test WHERE duration BETWEEN INTERVAL '0:30:00' AND INTERVAL '2:00:00';
SELECT id, duration FROM interval_test WHERE duration = (SELECT MAX(duration) FROM interval_test);
CREATE TABLE combined_test AS SELECT e.event_id, e.start_time, it.duration FROM events e JOIN interval_test it ON it.id = (e.event_id % 3) + 1;
SELECT AVG(duration) FROM combined_test WHERE duration > INTERVAL '1:00:00';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM interval_test WHERE duration IS NOT NULL;
DELETE FROM interval_test WHERE duration < INTERVAL '0:10:00';
